table_of_contents.title:
SIMPLE SYNTHETIC DATA REDUCES SYCOPHANCY IN
LARGE LANGUAGE MODELS
2 MODEL SCALING AND INSTRUCTION TUNING INCREASES SYCOPHANCY
3 MODELS ARE SYCOPHANTIC FOR OBJECTIVELY-WRONG ANSWERS

6 INTERVENTION REQUIRES FILTERING PROMPTS CONTAINING CLAIMS THE
MODEL DOES NOT KNOW THE ANSWER TO
A FURTHER EVALUATION OF SYNTHETIC-DATA INTERVENTION
A.1 SYNTHETIC-DATA INTERVENTION DOES NOT AFFECT PERFORMANCE ON BENCHMARKS
A.2 SYNTHETIC-DATA INTERVENTION DOES NOT AFFECT CHAIN-OF-THOUGHT REASONING
A.3 SYNTHETIC-DATA INTERVENTION DOES NOT AFFECT ZERO-SHOT PERFORMANCE
A.4 INTERVENTION DOES NOT AFFECT PRIOR KNOWLEDGE ON SYCOPHANCY TASKS
A.5 INTERVENTION REQUIRES MIXING INSTRUCTION-TUNING DATA
A.6 INTERVENTION ONLY REQUIRES A SMALL NUMBER OF FINETUNING STEPS
